overview: Orzhov Ephemerate is a Pauper midrange deck that leverages enter-the-battlefield (ETB) creatures to generate card advantage and board presence. By utilizing Ephemerate and Rescue the Foal, the deck replays these creatures to maximize their ETB triggers while maintaining initiative. Targeted removal and sweepers protect the board, allowing the deck to outvalue opponents through consistent value engines.
win_conditions: Populate the board with ETB creatures to draw cards and generate value
Replay creatures using Ephemerate and Rescue the Foal to maximize ETB triggers
Maintain initiative through consistent card draw and board presence
Use targeted removal and sweepers to clear opponent threats

keep_criteria: 1 to 3 creatures that draw cards (e.g., Thraben Inspector, Spirited Companion)
Ephemerate for combat tricks or protection
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably duals or fetches for W/B fixing), at least one turn-1 or turn-2 threat (Kor Skyfisher, Refurbished Familiar), or early interaction (Thraben Charm, Ephemerate).
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking card draw or board presence
Hands with too many high-cost cards without sufficient lands
Hands without removal or replay effects
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early plays, 3+ non-basic lands without fixing, or hands lacking any way to interact or develop a board.

examples.reason: Contains 2 lands, 2 card-drawing creatures, and Ephemerate for tricks/protection, matching the ideal hand description.

early_game: Play low-cost creatures to establish board presence and trigger ETB card draw
Use draw spells to find removal and replay effects
Turn 1: Play a land, optionally cast a cheap threat or hold for interaction.
Turn 2: Develop board with Kor Skyfisher or Refurbished Familiar, using Thraben Charm for lifegain, disruption, or temporary board stalls.
Sequence lands to ensure consistent access to both white and black mana for charms and instant-speed bounce.
mid_game: Cast targeted removal like Cast Down and Unmake to handle threats
Use Suffocating Fumes to clear boards of small creatures
Replay key creatures with Ephemerate or Rescue the Foal to regain initiative
Balance mana for interaction versus threat development.
Use the graveyard strategically for Refurbished Familiar recursion without overcommitting to it.
Avoid bouncing your own threats unless it triggers a specific value engine or buys critical tempo.
Commit threats when you have interaction to protect them or when the opponent lacks sweepers.
Hold back if facing heavy removal or board wipes.
Use Ephemerate and Thraben Charm to temporarily stall key threats while you stabilize or close out the game.
late_game: Maintain board control and initiative through consistent ETB value
Carefully manage resources in long games to avoid running out of answers
Kor Skyfisher (evading lifelink), Refurbished Familiar (value engine), and Thraben Charm modes.
Win by out-valuing through repeated interactions, cycling threats, or overwhelming with resilient, evasive pressure.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, protection, or the opponent is low on interaction.
Commit fully when you can close the game before they can stabilize or find a counterplay.

key_cards.name: Ephemerate
key_cards.role: Core replay effect and combat trick
key_cards.notes: Used to save creatures from removal or replay ETB triggers; avoid using it as an answer in the mirror.

key_cards.name: Pegasus Guardian
key_cards.notes: Provides Rescue the Foal, a 2-mana Ephemerate alternative that improves consistency.

key_cards.name: Thraben Inspector
key_cards.notes: Key creature for drawing cards upon entering the battlefield.

key_cards.name: Spirited Companion
key_cards.notes: Another primary creature for generating card advantage.

key_cards.name: Cast Down
key_cards.notes: Solves almost everything in the metagame.

key_cards.name: Unmake
key_cards.notes: Versatile removal spell to handle threats.

key_cards.name: Suffocating Fumes
key_cards.notes: Deals with small creatures; timing is crucial against Mono Blue Fairies.

key_cards.name: Bojuka Bog
key_cards.notes: Maindeck card that provides help against Dimir Terror.

sequencing_tips: Use Ephemerate as a combat trick or to protect creatures from removal rather than just as a direct answer
Prioritize playing and protecting flying creatures against Grixis Affinity
Time Suffocating Fumes carefully to maximize impact against Mono Blue Fairies
Avoid taking Ephemerate out of the sideboard in the mirror match
Conserve resources and play carefully in long matchups like the mirror or against Grixis Affinity
Common mistakes include over-bouncing your own threats without follow-up, mismanaging mana for Orzhov colors, and ignoring graveyard interactions.
Unique interactions: Thraben Charm modes can be sequenced for maximum value, and Refurbished Familiar synergizes with bounce effects for repeated triggers.
Always track opponent's hand and graveyard to optimize bounce and recursion timing.

matchups.advice: Stall the opponent in Game 1; the matchup improves in Games 2 and 3 with the sideboard.
Play a long game, prioritize flying creatures, and side in Dust to Dust.
Leverage Bojuka Bog in the main deck and side in Chainer's Edict and Relic of Progenitus to disrupt.
Play around their speed and counter-spells; time Suffocating Fumes carefully.
Play carefully, conserve resources, and avoid sideboarding out Ephemerate.

Use cheap threats, split casts, or bounce their counterspells with Ephemerate. Sequence Thraben Charm to disrupt their stack. Play around with timing and avoid overcommitting to single high-value spells.
Apply early pressure to disrupt their curve. Use artifact hate, graveyard hate, and Ephemerate to bounce ramp targets. Stabilize with lifelinked threats and efficient removal.

Prioritize early interaction, lifegain, and board wipes if available. Stabilize with lifelinked threats and efficient removal. Use bounce to temporarily stall key attackers while you set up your game plan.
Apply consistent pressure and use bounce to cycle threats. Out-value with card draw and recursion. Don't overcommit to one threat; force them to use multiple answers.
Value engine wins. Track graveyard, manage bounce timing, and out-resource with better sequencing. Prioritize keeping threats on the board and use Thraben Charm to disrupt their recursion.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells, sweepers, and combo pieces.
Note when opponents save removal for specific threats to plan your sequence.
Track graveyard content for recursion threats and monitor life totals to time lifelink-based wins.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Our strategy is made up of creatures that trigger an ability every time they come into play... This makes cards like Ephemerate great as we try to get the most out of ETB effects.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Our strategy is made up of creatures that trigger an ability every time they come into play... either by drawing cards, generating tokens, gaining life, making the opponent lose life and giving us the Initiative.
Rescue the Foal is a sort of Ephemerate for 2 mana, thus helping to have more copies of the same card, making our list achieve consistency.
always trying to mantain our Initiative , as it is one of the best ways to win the game.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: having Ephemerate as a combat trick or even to save our creatures from removals can be great for us to develop our game.
we always need to prioritize our creatures with flying, since this is the biggest problem for our opponent
try to fit a Suffocating Fumes at the right time.
Avoid taking Ephemerate as an answer.
be careful when spending resources, and especially know how to use them at the right time.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: be careful with Krark-Clan Shaman , as many of the Affinity decks run it and the damage to our board will be big.
we only value our opponent's Spellstutter Sprite
they all want the same thing: to win as fast as possible.
sweeper like Suffocating Fumes to deal with small creatures.
